heading: Definitions
label: 769 Definitions
notes: Change of NameReference to the Director of Central Intelligence or the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency in the Director’s capacity as the head of the intelligence community deemed to be a reference to the Director of National Intelligence. Reference to the Director of Central Intelligence or the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency in the Director’s capacity as the head of the Central Intelligence Agency deemed to be a reference to the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency. See section 1081(a), (b) of , set out as a note under , War and National Defense.Pub. L. 108–458section 3001 of Title 50

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

children.heading: INTELSAT
children.label: 1 INTELSAT
children.text: The term “INTELSAT” means the International Telecommunications Satellite Organization established pursuant to the Agreement Relating to the International Telecommunications Satellite Organization (INTELSAT).

children.heading: Inmarsat
children.label: 2 Inmarsat
children.text: The term “Inmarsat” means the International Mobile Satellite Organization established pursuant to the Convention on the International Maritime Organization.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of INTELSAT, or INTELSAT successors or separated entities, means a Party, or the telecommunications entity designated by a Party, that has signed the Operating Agreement and for which such Agreement has entered into force; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of Inmarsat, or Inmarsat successors or separated entities, means either a Party to, or an entity that has been designated by a Party to sign, the Operating Agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of INTELSAT, means a nation for which the INTELSAT agreement has entered into force; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of Inmarsat, means a nation for which the Inmarsat convention has entered into force.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 5 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.heading: International Telecommunication Union
children.label: 6 International Telecommunication Union
children.text: The term “International Telecommunication Union” means the intergovernmental organization that is a specialized agency of the United Nations in which member countries cooperate for the development of telecommunications, including adoption of international regulations governing terrestrial and space uses of the frequency spectrum as well as use of the geostationary satellite orbit.

children.label: A
children.text: means any privatized entity created from the privatization of INTELSAT or Inmarsat or from the assets of INTELSAT or Inmarsat; but

children.label: B
children.text: does not include any entity that is a separated entity.

children.heading: Separated entity
children.label: 8 Separated entity
children.text: The term “separated entity” means a privatized entity to whom a portion of the assets owned by INTELSAT or Inmarsat are transferred prior to full privatization of INTELSAT or Inmarsat, including in particular the entity whose structure was under discussion by INTELSAT as of , but excluding ICO.March 25, 1998

children.heading: Orbital location
children.label: 9 Orbital location
children.text: The term “orbital location” means the location for placement of a satellite on the geostationary orbital arc as defined in the International Telecommunication Union Radio Regulations.

children.heading: Space segment
children.label: 10 Space segment
children.text: The term “space segment” means the satellites, and the tracking, telemetry, command, control, monitoring and related facilities and equipment used to support the operation of satellites owned or leased by INTELSAT, Inmarsat, or a separated entity or successor entity.

children.heading: Non-core services
children.label: 11 Non-core services
children.text: The term “non-core services” means, with respect to INTELSAT provision, services other than public-switched network voice telephony and occasional-use television, and with respect to Inmarsat provision, services other than global maritime distress and safety services or other existing maritime or aeronautical services for which there are not alternative providers.

children.label: A
children.text: for Inmarsat, those non-maritime or non-aeronautical mobile services in the 1.5 and 1.6 Ghz band on planned satellites or the 2 Ghz band; and

children.label: B
children.text: for INTELSAT, direct-to-home (DTH) or direct broadcast satellite (DBS) video services, or services in the Ka or V bands.

children.heading: INTELSAT Agreement
children.label: 13 INTELSAT Agreement
children.text: The term “INTELSAT Agreement” means the Agreement Relating to the International Telecommunications Satellite Organization (“INTELSAT”), including all its annexes (TIAS 7532, 23 UST 3813).

children.heading: Headquarters Agreement
children.label: 14 Headquarters Agreement
children.text: The term “Headquarters Agreement” means the International Telecommunication Satellite Organization Headquarters Agreement () (TIAS 8542, 28 UST 2248).1So in original. Probably should be “Telecommunications”.1November 24, 1976

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of INTELSAT, the agreement, including its annex but excluding all titles of articles, opened for signature at Washington on , by Governments or telecommunications entities designated by Governments in accordance with the provisions of the Agreement; andAugust 20, 1971

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of Inmarsat, the Operating Agreement on the International Maritime Satellite Organization, including its annexes.

children.heading: Inmarsat Convention
children.label: 16 Inmarsat Convention
children.text: The term “Inmarsat Convention” means the Convention on the International Maritime Satellite Organization (Inmarsat) (TIAS 9605, 31 UST 1).

children.heading: National corporation
children.label: 17 National corporation
children.text: The term “national corporation” means a corporation the ownership of which is held through publicly traded securities, and that is incorporated under, and subject to, the laws of a national, state, or territorial government.

children.heading: COMSAT
children.label: 18 COMSAT
children.text: The term “COMSAT” means the corporation established pursuant to subchapter III of this chapter, or the successor in interest to such corporation.

children.heading: ICO
children.label: 19 ICO
children.text: The term “ICO” means the company known, as of , as ICO Global Communications, Inc.March 17, 2000

children.heading: Global maritime distress and safety services or GMDSS
children.label: 20 Global maritime distress and safety services or GMDSS
children.text: The term “global maritime distress and safety services” or “GMDSS” means the automated ship-to-shore distress alerting system which uses satellite and advanced terrestrial systems for international distress communications and promoting maritime safety in general. The GMDSS permits the worldwide alerting of vessels, coordinated search and rescue operations, and dissemination of maritime safety information.

children.heading: National security agency
children.label: 21 National security agency
children.text: The term “national security agency” means the National Security Agency, the Director of Central Intelligence and the Central Intelligence Agency, the Department of Defense, and the Coast Guard.

children.heading: Common terminology
children.label: b Common terminology
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in subsection (a), terms used in this subchapter that are defined in have the meanings provided in such section.section 153 of this title
